papers.title_en: Scalable Semantic Steering of Embedding Projections
papers.authors: Wei Liu, Eric Krokos, Kirsten Whitley, Rebecca Faust, Chris North
papers.evidence: 利用大语言模型对嵌入投影进行语义引导，涉及种子组聚类
papers.tldr: 低维嵌入投影的可视分析常因结构与分析师语义关系不一致而受限，现有LLM逐项推理方法成本随数据量线性增长。本文提出可扩展语义引导方法：仅需一次LLM调用，为所有用户定义组生成结构化概要，与种子质心融合为混合语义原型，再通过嵌入空间软分配、弃权和对齐缩放更新传播意图，无需重新训练。在5K文档LitCovid语料上，LLM调用减少超三个数量级，全局对齐效果与逐项法相当；图像案例表明该方法可扩展至多模态嵌入。组级语义表示显著降低计算成本，使语义引导更适用于大规模嵌入集合。
papers.abstract_en: Low-dimensional projections support interactive visual analysis of high-dimensional data embeddings, but their structure often does not align with analyst-defined semantic relationships. Recent LLM-augmented semantic steering methods address this gap by externalizing analyst intent from user-defined groups of seed examples, but they propagate intent through per-item LLM reasoning, causing LLM calls and cost to grow linearly with collection size. We propose a scalable semantic steering method that shifts semantic computation from individual items to user-defined groups. A single LLM call generates structured profiles for all groups, which are embedded and combined with seed centroids to form hybrid semantic prototypes. The method then propagates intent without retraining, using embedding-space soft assignment, abstention, and alignment-scaled updates before reprojection. On a 5K-document LitCovid corpus, our method achieves global alignment comparable to per-item LLM steering while reducing LLM calls by over three orders of magnitude. An image case study shows that the same prototype-based mechanism extends to multimodal embeddings. These results suggest that group-level representations can make semantic steering more practical for larger embedding collections.
